bottom_line: Add test_pending_migration_log_migration.py::test_downgrade_drops_table to the S01 deliverable list — the two-teardown spec missed the third migration file and caused a fix cycle at S09.
coverage_notes: S01–S03 logs read in full (< 2 KB each). S04–S07 first-run logs read in full (< 200 B); runs 2–4 are byte-identical. S08 run1: tail-50 + grep FAILED (362 KB); fix1 read in full; runs 3–5 tail-20. S09 run1: tail-30 + grep FAILED (360 KB); fix1 read in full; runs 3–4 tail-20. S10 run1: head-20 + tail-20 + grep errors (78 KB); fix1 is 0 bytes; run3 tail-10. S11 read in full. All logs under 1 MB. DB telemetry: full (iw item-status --json).

findings.title: S01 prompt named two migration teardowns but test_pending_migration_log_migration.py needed a third
findings.recommendation: Extend S01 deliverable (5) to name all three migration files with a downgrade step that need a post-downgrade upgrade call: test_oss_migration.py, test_project_oss_job_migration.py, and test_pending_migration_log_migration.py. The 4-seed spike missed this because no tested seed reordered test_downgrade_drops_table before the column/constraint tests.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00055_S09_fix1.log:1 — 'adding command.upgrade(alembic_cfg, "head") after the downgrade assertion … the two sibling migration test files [had the same pattern]'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add test_pending_migration_log_migration.py::test_downgrade_drops_table to the S01 deliverable teardown list — missed in CR-00055 S01 prompt, caused S09 fix cycle; see ai-dev/active/CR-00055/reports/CR-00055_self_assess_report.md [1]. Target: CR_Design_Template or R-00077 Appendix A. Effort: S.

findings.title: Live config file (executor/auto_merge.toml) in a unit test broke S08 due to parallel-initiative commit on the branch
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Pre-conditions / Branch State' note to the CR design template instructing authors to list any non-CR commits on the branch that modify live config files exercised by unit tests (e.g., executor/auto_merge.toml). Alternatively add a pre-S01 checklist item: 'Run git log --oneline to identify any config-file commits from parallel initiatives that may render existing assertions stale.'
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00055_S08_fix1.log:1 — 'test was asserting config.phase == PHASE_DISABLED (0), but executor/auto_merge.toml was advanced to phase = 1 (PHASE_DRY_RUN) via commit 1856cf8b'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add 'Pre-conditions / Branch State' section to CR design template to flag parallel-initiative commits that change live config files; diagnosed in CR-00055 S08 fix cycle; see ai-dev/active/CR-00055/reports/CR-00055_self_assess_report.md [2]. Target: templates/design/CR_Design_Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Flaky docker-startup test in diff-coverage gate (S10) triggered empty fix cycle
findings.recommendation: Add startup-retry logic to test_two_parallel_iw_ai_core_worktrees_do_not_interfere, or quarantine it with @pytest.mark.flaky(reruns=2), or open an Incident to track this known flaky docker-stack test. The S10/fix1 log is 0 bytes — no code change was applied — confirming the failure was transient.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00055_S10_fix1.log — 0 bytes (empty; no code change applied)
ai-dev/logs/CR-00055_S10_run3.log (tail) — '2522 passed … Completed CR-00055 step S10' (passed without any fix)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident test_two_parallel_iw_ai_core_worktrees_do_not_interfere is flaky on docker-stack startup and blocked CR-00055 S10 diff-coverage gate; fix cycle was empty; test passed on retry. Add startup retry or flaky marker. Target: tests/integration/test_per_worktree_isolation.py. Effort: S.
